skill.name: dbos-golang
skill.description: Guidance for building reliable Go applications with DBOS durable workflows, steps, queues, clients, testing, and workflow communication patterns.
skill.summary: Build reliable Go applications with DBOS workflows, steps, queues, and durable execution patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but semantic review shows they are Markdown and Go documentation examples. No prompt injection, malicious execution path, obfuscation, or secret exfiltration was found; residual risk is low because examples mention installation commands, URLs, and database environment variables.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution matches are Markdown false positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches occur in Markdown tables, inline code, file lists, and Go examples. I found no executable Ruby, shell wrapper, spawn call, or user-controlled command construction in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences, table entries, or documentation paths. Semantic review found documentation only, not runtime command execution.

low_findings.title: Examples include legitimate network and database configuration
low_findings.description: The skill contains Go examples that read DBOS database URLs from environment variables and call example HTTP endpoints. These are normal DBOS application patterns and do not exfiltrate marketplace or agent secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment and HTTP access appears inside instructional Go snippets for user applications. No code sends environment values to an external destination.

low_findings.title: Critical heuristic dismissed after semantic review
low_findings.description: The scanner combined command, network, and credential-access signals across unrelated Markdown examples. I found no malicious code path, obfuscation, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or executable installer logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant files are documentation and Go snippets. The SHA-256 mention is a secure hash description, not weak cryptography or obfuscation.

content.user_title: Build Durable Go Workflows with DBOS
content.value_statement: Reliable Go services need workflows that survive retries, crashes, and deployment changes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through DBOS workflow, step, queue, and client patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to configure DBOS contexts, register workflows, and launch Go applications.
Guides workflow and step design for deterministic durable execution.
Covers DBOS queues, concurrency limits, rate limiting, partitioning, priority, and deduplication.
Describes workflow communication through events, messages, streams, and background workflows.
Provides testing, idempotency, versioning, patching, scheduling, and timeout guidance.
limitations: It is documentation guidance, not a runnable DBOS project generator by itself.
Examples use placeholder endpoints and database environment variables that users must configure.
It does not replace current DBOS release notes or official API references.
It focuses on Go and does not cover DBOS SDKs for other languages.

use_cases.title: Add durable workflows to a Go service
use_cases.description: Use DBOS configuration, workflow registration, and step patterns when converting fragile request logic into durable execution.

use_cases.title: Control asynchronous job processing
use_cases.description: Apply queue concurrency, rate limit, partition, priority, and deduplication guidance for safer background workloads.

use_cases.title: Review DBOS application design
use_cases.description: Check determinism, idempotency, testing, patching, and workflow communication choices before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new DBOS Go workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dbos-golang skill to outline a basic Go workflow with DBOS context setup, registration, launch, and one durable step.

prompt_templates.title: Move side effects into steps
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve workflow determinism in existing code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Go workflow with the dbos-golang skill. Identify non-deterministic logic and move external calls into DBOS steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a queued workload
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan worker throughput and duplicate prevention.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dbos-golang skill to design a DBOS queue for this workload. Include concurrency, rate limiting, priority, and deduplication guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an advanced DBOS rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dbos-golang skill to audit this DBOS Go design for idempotency, patching, versioning, testing, timeouts, and workflow communication.

output_examples.input: Review a Go workflow that calls an external API directly inside workflow code.
output_examples.output: The review explains that external API calls belong in DBOS steps, then lists the corrected workflow structure and retry considerations.

output_examples.input: Plan a queue for order processing with strict rate limits and duplicate prevention.
output_examples.output: The response recommends queue options for concurrency, rate limiting, priority, and deduplication, with notes on workflow IDs.

output_examples.input: Prepare a safe deployment for a workflow that changes step order.
output_examples.output: The response describes DBOS patching, application version handling, and the sequence for deprecating old workflow paths.

best_practices: Register workflows and queues before launching the DBOS application.
Keep workflow code deterministic and place side effects inside DBOS steps.
Use workflow IDs, queue deduplication, and tests to prevent duplicate side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not run external API calls directly inside workflow logic.
Do not enqueue workflows from inside steps unless DBOS documentation supports the pattern.
Do not deploy breaking workflow step changes without versioning or patching guidance.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants write and review Go applications that use DBOS durable workflows, steps, queues, and clients.

faq.question: Does it run DBOS commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users decide which commands or code changes to apply.

faq.question: Can it help with existing Go code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review workflow determinism, step boundaries, queue usage, testing setup, and deployment safety.

faq.question: Does it cover DBOS queues?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers queue basics, concurrency, rate limiting, partitioning, priority, deduplication, and queue-specific workers.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes unit tests with mocked DBOS contexts and integration tests with a Postgres database.

faq.question: Is it limited to Go?
faq.answer: Yes. The references and examples are for the DBOS Go SDK.
